summary: Frontend-only structural/functional testing of Method marketing site. All 15 review scenarios pass. Nav, routing, testids, mailto links, footer visibility rules, mobile nav, typography (Scandia + Cormorant Garamond), and no-forms guarantee are all working. Zero non-third-party console errors.
critical_code_review_comments: Nav.jsx line 23-25: useEffect that resets mobileOpen has empty dependency array — it only runs on mount, so it does nothing useful. If intent was to close panel on route change, dependency should be [pathname] via useLocation(). Currently the panel closes via explicit onClick handlers on each mobile link, which works, but the useEffect is dead code that should be removed or fixed.
App.js catch-all route `path='*'` renders Home component instead of a proper 404 page. Consider a NotFound component for better UX (minor).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Static marketing site — no backend integration. Testing focused on: (a) presence of all required data-testid attributes across 7 pages, (b) mailto: hrefs, (c) footer visibility (present everywhere except /connect), (d) no forms/inputs/submit-buttons anywhere, (e) mobile hamburger menu open/close/navigate, (f) typography families computed correctly. Note the writing-featured element also carries a writing-card-<slug> testid — this is intentional and matches spec (8 total writing-card entries = 8 total posts). Also note connect-email-primary mailto href includes a ?subject= prefill parameter — this is enhancement, not a bug. Home hero uses a two-column layout on desktop; wordmark (left) and italic tagline (right) share vertical space but are horizontally separated — verified via bounding-box X-axis check. Frontend URL: https://method-positioning.preview.emergentagent.com
